news.title: Delhi civic body to install CCTV cameras at 524 spots to tackle construction dumping
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) is installing CCTV cameras at 524 locations to combat illegal dumping of construction and demolition waste. This initiative aims to identify and penalize habitual offenders, complementing existing designated dumping sites and processing plants. The city generates substantial C&D waste daily, with efforts underway to increase processing capacity and improve waste management infrastructure.

news.title: ED arrests ex-MD of Jaypee Infratech in ₹14,599 crore 'scam' case
news.para: In a significant move, the Enforcement Directorate apprehended Manoj Gaur, the former Managing Director of Jaypee Infratech, as part of a massive fraud investigation concerning ₹14,599 crore. This scandal involves the misuse of funds intended for housing projects, with allegations that home buyers' investments were misappropriated.

news.title: Sub-registrar, realtor arrested in land-grab case in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru CCB sleuths have arrested a sub-registrar and a realtor-cum-middleman in a land-grab case involving forged documents and the sale of multiple residential sites without the landowner's knowledge. The August 2022 case, initially filed at Parappana Agrahara police station, has led to the arrest of approximately 100 suspects so far.

news.title: Telangana RERA directs Aliens Developers to refund ₹2 lakh to homebuyer
news.para: Telangana RERA has ordered Aliens Developers Pvt Ltd to refund ₹2 lakh to a homebuyer within 30 days. The buyer had paid a booking advance for a flat in the 'Aliens Space Station' project and received a partial refund after cancellation. The developer's claim of full refund and contractual dispute was dismissed by RERA.

news.title: After HC order on illegal colonies in Jaipur, residents, developers seek JDA action
news.para: Jaipur authorities face a High Court order to clear illegal constructions on government land. Residents and developers are demanding strict action against encroachments in Sanganer tehsil. Showrooms, boutiques, and eateries operate without permission, obstructing planned development and public safety.

news.title: State government opens 120-day online window to regularise illegal buildings in Guntur
news.para: Guntur residents can now regularize unauthorized constructions through the Building Penalisation Scheme (BPS), with applications accepted online for 120 days. The government offers concessions for older buildings and exemptions for small residential structures, encouraging property owners to comply with regulations and avoid demolition.

news.title: Charges framed against SRS Group promoters in ₹2,200 crore money laundering case
news.para: A PMLA court has formally charged SRS Group promoters and officials in a ₹2,200-crore money laundering case investigated by the ED. The group allegedly lured investors with high returns, then diverted funds into shell companies for unrelated ventures. This development marks a step towards justice for duped homebuyers and investors.

news.title: ED searches 14 sites linked to ex-director of Lodha Developers, aides
news.para: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted searches at 14 locations in Mumbai and surrounding areas linked to former Lodha Developers director Rajendra Lodha and his associates. Lodha is accused of money laundering, including fraudulently selling flats and misusing company assets, leading to losses for the Lodha Group. The ED's case stems from an FIR registered by the city crime branch.

news.title: BPTP eyes listing of its real estate business
news.para: Real estate firm BPTP Ltd is reportedly seeking a merchant banker to list its business, aiming to tap into a booming IPO market. With a substantial land bank and ambitious project launches planned, the company anticipates significant revenue growth. This move aligns with a broader trend of increased real estate listings and fundraising in recent years.

news.title: HDFC Capital Advisors makes its second exit from Adarsh Developers
news.para: HDFC Capital Advisors has exited its second investment in Adarsh Developers. The partnership has successfully supported Adarsh Developers in repaying debt. HDFC Capital's strategy focuses on partnering with top developers to finance quality homes for mid-income households. Both entities are expected to continue their collaboration on remaining projects.

news.title: Bengaluru: BDA ends 20-year Arkavathy row, allots 784 sites at Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout
news.para: After a two-decade wait, the BDA has allotted 784 alternative plots in Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout to Arkavathy Layout allottees who lost their sites due to land denotification. This computerised randomisation process marks a significant resolution, providing long-overdue relief and restoring faith in the system for many beneficiaries.

news.title: Noida authority restores rehab policy benefits to Sikka Infrastructure cancelled over ₹252 crore dues
news.para: Noida Authority granted in-principle approval to Sikka Infrastructure for legacy stalled project benefits for its Sikka Karnam Greens project. This relief is contingent on the builder withdrawing a pending revision petition and fulfilling conditions, including providing unsold units as a financial guarantee to facilitate funding from the SWAMIH Investment Fund.

news.title: Alta Capital checks into Isprava's luxury homes with $100 million investment
news.para: Isprava Group, a leader in Goa's luxury real estate, is set to receive an Rs 850-crore investment from Alta Capital. This significant funding will fuel expansion into popular vacation spots like Goa and Alibaug. The investment will also enable international offerings for ultra-rich clients and provide exits for early investors. Alta Capital will become a major shareholder post-transaction.

news.title: Construction ban imposed in Noida as GRAP stage-III comes into force
news.para: In an urgent bid to tackle the alarming pollution crisis in the Delhi-NCR area, Noida has implemented rigorous restrictions on air quality. Only essential construction projects remain active, while all other work has been halted. The regulations extend to a ban on BS-III petrol and BS-IV diesel vehicles, along with the closure of ready-mix concrete plants and stone crushers.

news.title: Pune Jain property row: Sale nullified but cancellation deed pending
news.para: A significant sale of prime property in Pune owned by a Jain trust is hitting a snag as delays continue to mount. The trust and builder remain silent on formalizing the cancellation, stoking tensions among locals.

news.title: Repco Home Finance's net profit falls 5.12% in Q2 FY26
news.para: Repco Home Finance reported a 5.12% dip in net consolidated profit to ₹103.15 crore for Q2 FY26, despite a 4.18% rise in total income to ₹441.90 crore. The company's loan book grew 7.7% to ₹15,033 crore, with GNPA ratio improving to 3.16%.

news.title: Sweden's SBB to offload community property portfolio in deal valued at $3.4 billion
news.para: In a strategic pivot, Swedish real estate powerhouse SBB has put its community property portfolio on the auction block for a staggering 32 billion Swedish crowns. This decisive action is designed to alleviate SBB's hefty debt load.

news.title: Ashiana Housing posts net profit of ₹27.54 crore in Q2 FY26
news.para: Ashiana Housing reported a net consolidated profit of ₹27.54 crore in Q2 FY26, a significant turnaround from a loss of ₹7.55 crore in the previous year. The company's total income surged by 195.95% to ₹176.18 crore. Ashiana Housing also acquired land in Chennai for a new senior living project with substantial sales potential.

news.title: Kolkata: NKDA issues notice to 13 owners for commercial use of homes
news.para: New Town authorities have issued notices to owners of 13 residential buildings for operating unauthorized commercial facilities. These properties were being used as guest houses, restaurants, offices, and pre-schools, posing fire hazards and violating building plans. This action follows earlier notices issued to other property owners.

news.title: SRG Housing Finance's net profit rises 24.85% in Q2 FY26
news.para: SRG Housing Finance has announced a robust 24.85% surge in net profit for the September quarter of FY26, reaching ₹8.24 crore. Total income also climbed significantly by 31.49% to ₹48.44 crore. The company's Assets Under Management (AUM) grew by approximately 33% year-on-year, demonstrating sustained growth and strong portfolio quality.
